name: Industrial Software / OT Stack
description: Cross-cutting industrial software, OT, automation, simulation and data stack extracted from AI, defence, aerospace and manufacturing realities.

name: CAD / CAM Layer
description: Mechanical design, manufacturing programming, NC/CNC, tooling, product geometry and engineering authoring.

name: CAE / Simulation Layer
description: Physics simulation, systems simulation, finite element analysis, CFD, electromagnetic simulation and virtual validation.

name: PLM / ALM / MBSE Layer
description: Requirements, product lifecycle, bill of materials, configuration, change management, ALM and model-based systems engineering.

name: MES / MOM Layer
description: Work orders, production execution, quality, traceability, genealogy, shop-floor performance and manufacturing operations management.

name: SCADA / HMI Layer
description: Supervisory control, operator interfaces, alarms, historian connections and plant applications.

name: PLC / Control Runtime Layer
description: PLCs, industrial controllers, runtime systems, motion control, safety systems and control programming.

name: Industrial Connectivity Layer
description: Protocol gateways, OPC UA, MQTT/Sparkplug, fieldbus bridges and IT/OT connectivity.

name: Industrial DataOps / Contextualization Layer
description: Time-series data, asset models, semantic context, namespace, event streams and operational data products.

name: Digital Twin / Virtual Twin Layer
description: Virtual twins of products, plants, lines, assets, buildings and operational systems.

name: Industrial AI / Agent Layer
description: Industrial copilots, optimization agents, anomaly detection, predictive maintenance, planning and engineering automation.

name: OT Cybersecurity Layer
description: Asset discovery, network monitoring, vulnerability management, segmentation, incident response and IEC 62443 evidence.

name: Industrial Cloud / Edge Layer
description: Industrial edge compute, cloud IoT, device management, edge workloads and data pipelines.

name: ERP / Supply Chain Integration Layer
description: ERP, supply chain planning, procurement, production planning and enterprise-to-shop-floor integration.

name: Maintenance / Reliability Layer
description: CMMS, EAM, predictive maintenance, asset performance, work orders and reliability analytics.

name: Industrial Standards / Governance Layer
description: ISA/IEC 62443, ISA-95, OPC UA, MQTT Sparkplug and governance patterns that make industrial data portable and secure.

name: Frontline Operations / Worker Layer
description: Work instructions, operator guidance, inspections, remote assistance, training, EHS and frontline apps.

name: Industrial Low-Code / App Layer
description: Low-code apps, dashboards, workflows, forms and data-driven plant applications.

name: Quality / Traceability Layer
description: Batch records, genealogy, nonconformance, serialization, audit trails and traceability across regulated manufacturing.

name: OPC UA
description: Platform-independent architecture for secure industrial interoperability and information modeling.

name: MQTT Sparkplug
description: Standard for MQTT topic namespace and payloads for industrial data producers and consumers.

name: ISA-95
description: Enterprise-control system integration standard structuring ERP, MES and control layers.

name: ISA/IEC 62443
description: Consensus-based standards for secure industrial automation and control systems.

name: Unified Namespace
description: Shared namespace architecture for contextual industrial data streams and OT/IT integration.

name: Digital Thread
description: Connected requirements, design, simulation, manufacturing, quality and service evidence across lifecycle.

name: Industrial Ontology / Asset Model
description: Common operational data model connecting assets, tags, events, processes, products and systems.

name: Brownfield Integration Gap
description: Factories contain old machines, proprietary protocols, heterogeneous PLCs and manual workflows; integration is expensive and brittle.

name: IT / OT Semantic Gap
description: Enterprise data teams often lack meaningful asset, process, unit, product and operational context for OT data.

name: Industrial Vendor Lock-in
description: Plants often depend on vendor-specific tools, proprietary formats, licenses, protocols and integrator knowledge.

name: OT Cyber Asset Visibility Gap
description: Industrial operators often lack complete OT asset inventory, vulnerability context and secure remote access visibility.

name: Industrial Data Quality Gap
description: Noisy tags, missing metadata, bad timestamps, inconsistent units and poor lineage make industrial analytics unreliable.

name: Digital Thread Breakage
description: Requirements, CAD, simulation, manufacturing, quality and service evidence break across tools and organizations.

name: Simulation-to-Shopfloor Gap
description: Simulation and digital twin outputs are often disconnected from MES, PLC, quality and operator workflows.

name: Industrial AI Trust Gap
description: Industrial AI requires evidence, guardrails, root-cause explanations, safe deployment and operator trust.

name: Frontline Adoption Gap
description: Operator adoption fails when software ignores real shop-floor workflows, offline realities, shift patterns and maintenance habits.

name: IEC 62443 Evidence Gap
description: IEC 62443 requirements create need for continuous evidence, patch status, segmentation, policies and vendor accountability.

name: Factory Energy Visibility Gap
description: Plants need machine/line/process-level energy and emissions visibility, not only utility bills or corporate reports.

name: Industrial Change Management Gap
description: Code, recipes, BOMs, PLC programs, machine parameters and process changes often lack unified audit/control.

name: EU AI Stack Reference
description: Industrial software layer supports EU AI opportunities through data, deployment, edge, governance and AI factories.

name: Global AI Stack Reference
description: Industrial software layer depends on and feeds global AI stack through industrial data, edge, inference and model routing.

name: European Defence Stack Reference
description: Industrial software layer supports defence readiness, supplier visibility, simulation, compliance and secure integration.

name: Global Aerospace Stack Reference
description: Industrial software layer supports aerospace certification, digital thread, MRO, quality and supplier visibility.
